narrative.problem: 5G network slicing SLA admission must be preventive, multidomain-aware, and evidence-backed, yet runtime systems often overclaim closed-loop causality across orchestration, core, and lifecycle paths.
narrative.hypothesis: A digest-frozen TriSLA runtime can deliver preventive SLA-aware admission with PRB-governed score_mode while characterizing—rather than assuming—causal boundaries for orchestration, core, and lifecycle subsystems.
narrative.contribution_summary: Preventive admission architecture + formal negative characterization of non-causal paths.
narrative.results_summary: Proven preventive admission, persistence, detached orchestration, reconciliation; negative: no robust admission divergence, no orch/core/lifecycle closed-loop causality.
narrative.limitations_summary: Fragmented persistence, no orch→DE feedback, no continuous reevaluation, NAD liminal gap.
narrative.future_work_summary: Callbacks, unified SLA registry, continuous SLA-Agent loop; research on causal multidomain.

caution_expressions: slice-specific admission (qualify: score proven; label divergence limited)
orchestration lifecycle continuity (qualify: metadata + CRD; not closed-loop)
multidomain runtime (qualify: observable/contributive; not balanced causal)
feasibility-aware admission (qualify: snapshot at decision time)
standards alignment (qualify: conceptual references only; not normative compliance)

contributions.title: Preventive SLA-aware admission
contributions.writing: We demonstrate preventive admission via score_mode with PRB hard gates under a frozen container digest (n=450 NASP-hard+).

contributions.title: PRB-governed runtime model
contributions.writing: Resource pressure uses frozen resource_pressure_v1 (0.4·PRB + 0.3·RTT + 0.3·CPU) at decision time, not post-orchestration feedback.

contributions.title: Semantic persistence
contributions.writing: SEM-CSMF SQLite stores intents, nests, and slices enabling semantic replay independent of portal response ephemera.

contributions.title: Reconciliatory orchestration
contributions.writing: NASP executes detached orchestration (Portal→instantiate→CRD) with NSI watch and 60s reconciler (10k+ reservations).

contributions.title: Immutable governance
contributions.writing: BC-NSSMF records governance lineage at submit; immutable relative to continuous SLA-Agent loops.

contributions.title: Characterization of causal limits
contributions.writing: Cross-track evidence (NAD, CORE, NCM, LIFE, ORCH) formalizes non-causal boundaries as scientific contribution.

positive_results: Tri-slice score differentiation under same network_state_id (score_mode)
Runtime persistence across SEM, CRD, and BC
Detached orchestration with measurable HTTP latency under contention (~9×)
negative_results: No robust tri-slice admission divergence at frozen guards (NAD)
No orchestration→DE callback or pressure/score feedback (ORCH)
No core-driven admission effect under contention (CORE)
No continuous autonomous reevaluation (LIFE; SLA-Agent Kafka unwired)

abstract.draft_sentence: TriSLA provides digest-frozen preventive SLA-aware admission with PRB-governed score_mode and empirically characterizes non-causal orchestration, core, and lifecycle paths.

forbidden: we achieve full autonomous SLA governance
feedback loop from NSI to DE

paper_structure.purpose: Problem, method, key proven + negative results, contribution
SLA admission, slicing orchestration, negative-results precedent
Negative results as contribution; comparison to overclaiming literature
Preventive admission proven; causal closure not claimed

mandatory_questions.Q2_detail: Forbidden expressions catalogued; camera-ready sections validated
mandatory_questions.Q4_detail: Framed as rigorous characterization, not experimental failure
mandatory_questions.Q5_detail: NON_CAUSAL for orch/core/lifecycle/multidomain; PREVENTIVE for admission only
